source-discipline.reason: No lang_jur_map row is written; the DR explicitly refuses to re-populate the table (which was withdrawn as fabrication in DR-2026-06-11) and instead proposes the missing role definitions for owner ratification, with a per-row sourcing standard grounded in verifiable official-language facts. Fabrication risk is named and structurally avoided by writing nothing.

jurisdictional-synthesis.reason: The DR defines the jurisdiction x language role taxonomy (PRIMARY/SECONDARY) that bridges the language and jurisdiction coverage axes, with a [PRIMARY-LANGUAGE-GAP] flag for jurisdictions whose authoring language falls outside the 19 — surfacing under-reachability rather than dropping it.

adherence-logging-and-attestation.reason: Commit carries [DOCTRINE: 0f2f525] before the timestamp per rule #11(a); this attestation is co-committed per rule #11(b). doctrine_sha matches HEAD:governance/mission-and-epistemics.md.

bias_direction: Risk of quietly repeating the withdrawn 2026-06-11 auto-population under a new name — an agent supplying jurisdiction/language scope that is owner-only (DG-NON). Mitigated by writing zero rows, dropping the undefined COLONIAL role entirely, defining PRIMARY/SECONDARY before proposing any assignment, and routing the whole map to the owner as a proposal rather than a migration; a second reviewer of the actual row set is required before any land.
independent_reviewer_counterclaim: A reviewer could argue that even proposing PRIMARY/SECONDARY assignments pre-commits the owner to a colonial-inflected framing and should not be drafted by an agent at all. Counter: the DR proposes only the definitions and a sourcing standard, flags the erasure risk explicitly via [PRIMARY-LANGUAGE-GAP], commits no rows, and states in three places that jurisdiction/language scope is the owner's DG-NON call — the agent supplies the missing definitions migration 023 asked for, nothing more.
